rulings.Case Title: SUSAN PETERSON et al VS. AC AND S, INC et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 25, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANT HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF BECAUSE THE HEIR'S LACK OF KNOWLEDGE FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THEY CANNOT ESTABLISH LIABILITY. WEBER V JOHN CRANE, INC. (2006) 143 CAL APP 4TH 1433. THE COURT STRIKES THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED WITH THE REPLY. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN PETERSON et al VS. AC AND S, INC et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 25, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANT FLOWERSERVE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. THE SPENCE DECLARATION FAILS TO PROVIDE ANY FACTS IN PARAGRAPHS ONE AND TWO FOR THE COURT TO BE ABLE TO CONCLUDE THAT HE CAN TESTIFY TO THE FACTS IN PARAGRAPH SEVEN. IN ADDITION, THE ABSENCE OF RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND THE TESTIMONY OF THE EXISTENCE OF CO-WORKERS ARE FURTHER EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLEY BROWN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 25, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANT LESLIE CONTROLS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT CONCEDES THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT THAT PRECLUDE THE GRANTING OF THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION. THE LATE FILING OF THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES PRECLUDES CONSIDERATION OF THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF INTENTIONAL TORT AND PUNITIVE DAMAGE CLAIMS. ABSENT THE INTERROGATORIES, THE BURDEN IS NOT SHIFTED. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLEY BROWN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 25, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANT ELLIOTT COMPANY'S, FORMERLY KNOWN AS "ELLIOTT TURBOMACHINERY CO., INC.", MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF BASED ON FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY. THE COURT SUSTAINS OBJECTIONS TWO THROUGH FIFTEEN TO THE BROWN DECLARATION BASED ON D'AMICO AND HEARSAY. ALTHOUGH THE DEPOSITION QUESTIONS REGARDING ELLIOTT WERE FEW IN NUMBER, PLAINTIFF'S LACK OF KNOWLEDGE AS TO ELLIOTT WAS REAFFIRMED IN THE RESPONSE TO THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. IN THESE CIRCUMSTANCES, STRIKING THE DECLARATION BASED ON D'AMICO IS WARRANTED, PARTICULARLY GIVEN PLAINTIFF'S ABILITY TO IDENTIFY OTHER PUMP MANUFACTURERS IN THE COURSE OF DISCOVERY. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD EDDY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 25, 2008 LINE 10. DEFENDANT LAMONS GASKET COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof based on plaintiff's factually devoid discovery which fails to set forth a basis on which plaintiff was exposed to a product manufactured, sold, distributed or produced by Power Engineering or Lammons. Plaintiff's evidence does not create a triable issue of fact. All plaintiff is able to do is to establish that plaintiff worked with Flexitallic gaskets but plaintiff is unable to offer any evidence that Power Engineering sold the gaskets that plaintiff was exposed to. The fact that plaintiff was on a ship for three months in California at a naval base does not show that he was there during the period that Power Engineering was the exclusive distributor or that plaintiff did any work on such gaskets at Mare Island or that any Flexitallic gaskets on board the USS Tulare were supplied by Power Engineering. Given this ruling, the Court need not address the issue of successor liability. Defendant's objection to Power Engineering's GO 129 interrogatories is overruled. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: WARREN HARRINGTON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 25, 2008 LINE 11. DEFENDANT COOPER CAMERON CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. THE JOHNSON DECLARATION LACKS FOUNDATION FOR THE STATEMENT IN PARAGRAPH TWO. PLAINTIFF'S TESTIMONY AS TO THE USE OF THE PRODUCT HAS NOT BEEN REBUTTED AND ALSO CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: VANESSA-RENE MONTANO VS. JAMES READ SKAGGS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, APRIL 25, 2008 LINE 16. DEFENDANT JAMES SKAGGS'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment IS GRANTED. THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED BY THE PARTIES IS CONFLICTING AS TO DEFENDANT'S PLACE OF RESIDENCE AT THE TIME OF SERVICE. ON BALANCE, THE RECORD SUPPORTS A FINDING THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT RESIDE AT HIS MOTHER'S HOME. GIVEN THAT THE RECORD IS AMBIGUIOUS AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT HAD ACTUAL NOTICE OF THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT, RELIEF FROM DEFAULT IS GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. SECTION 473.5. THE LAW STRONGLY FAVORS TRIAL ON THE MERITS. =(302/PJM/AY)
